deals.name: CLS Health
deals.champion: Abigail Escalante (EMR Ops Manager); President of CLS also engaged
deals.status: Pricing approved internally (Teresa, Apr 20) at $3,500/mo for 3,000 telemed visits. On the Apr 23 pricing call, Abigail was surprised by the dual-fee model — flat platform fee plus SolvPay usage fees — raising concern about $18K in fees over 6 months before telehealth revenue generates. She is requesting a hybrid pricing model, 6-month initial term, and waived or reduced $2K implementation fee. Luma confirmed unable to match Solv's full A-to-Z capability, making Solv the only viable option. President is applying internal pressure to move fast; ideal launch target is June 1.
key_risks: Dual-fee pricing model causing sticker shock; concessions likely needed
ECW to Epic/Oracle migration possible in ~1 year — contract flexibility required
Pre-visit payment collection workflow for telehealth still unconfirmed
deals.next_step: Brantley + Teresa to discuss pricing flexibility with finance (1–2 days); consider getting President on a call; confirm June 1 start feasibility with implementation team.
deals.key_notes: Pricing approved but dual-fee model is creating active objection — finance flexibility decision in 1–2 days.

deals.name: Great Lakes Orthopaedic Center (GLOC)
deals.size: 2 locations to start, ~2,000 calls/week; 40 physicians + 100 PAs after acquisitions
deals.champion: Jim (CEO); Deanna (Clinical Manager) skeptical but involved
deals.status: Pricing proposal delivered Apr 21. Teresa finalized structure: free pilot → $1.25/resolved call + $0.40/ClearPay at scale. Kapeesh flagged that Jim needs to see eventual pricing and ROI before deciding whether the pilot is worth his time at all. Jim must build a defensible cost model to present to his physician partners before committing. GLOC just tripled in size via two acquisitions, complicating the scheduling environment. No ortho reference customer remains the top objection.
key_risks: No ortho reference customer — Jim explicitly does not want to be a beta test
ClearPay value unclear vs. $100K/month Athena spend (perceived overlap)
Collections is a culture problem — physicians waive fees; Jim acknowledged leadership change needed internally
deals.next_step: Brantley and Teresa to follow up on Jim's timeline for presenting to partners; prep ortho-specific ROI model for war room.
deals.key_notes: Free pilot offer sent; Jim needs a defensible ROI model before he'll commit — partner consensus required.

deals.name: Total Primary Care
deals.status: Deprioritized as of Apr 7 — 6 to 9 month horizon. Apr 1 discovery confirmed Total Primary Care will not replace their CTO-built custom scheduler. Rikin proposed Solv purely as a new-patient acquisition channel: patients find Total Primary Care via Solv.com, book through Solv, sync into ECW, with all existing workflows untouched. Maya interest is lukewarm — they are already piloting a competing voice agent. Solv's internal appetite for an acquisition-only model with no payments or paperwork is unconfirmed.
key_risks: CTO-built scheduler is locked in — full Solv deployment not possible
Maya competitor already in active pilot; ECW integration skepticism remains
Solv's willingness to support an acquisition-only model (no payments, no paperwork) is undecided
deals.next_step: Internal Solv go/no-go decision needed on acquisition-only model; if yes, schedule technical call with Total Primary Care CTO.
deals.key_notes: Won't replace custom scheduler; acquisition-only model needs internal Solv green light before any re-engagement.

deals.name: Premier Ortho
deals.champion: Jon Borja (COO) — proactive, confirmed intent to return
deals.status: Formally paused since Mar 11. Jon messaged proactively (rather than ghosting) to explain that contractual obligations to Assort block all movement with no exit timeline. He confirmed intent to return when clear. No new signals since. Brantley checking in every ~2 weeks.
key_risks: Assort contract exit timeline unknown — could drag indefinitely
Urgency and momentum fade the longer the pause extends
Zero visibility or leverage on the Assort negotiation
deals.next_step: Brantley periodic check-in every ~2 weeks; Jon holds the ball.
deals.key_notes: Paused — blocked by Assort contract with no exit timeline; Jon self-advocating to return when clear.

deals.name: Elevate Health Group
deals.champion: Katia Sarfian (Administrator); Dr. Tovmassian (owner) is the blocker
deals.status: Near-dead. Dr. Tovmassian rejected on Mar 16: 'Doesn't make sense for our practice, 90% of patients are recurring.' ClearPay's point-of-care estimate workflow does not fit a recurring primary care base. Follow-up sent Mar 24 requesting a leadership call; no response. Brantley not pushing hard to protect the existing Mederi relationship with the same owner.
key_risks: Owner is the decision maker and has rejected the current offer
ClearPay product-market fit gap for recurring primary care patients is real
Aggressive outreach risks damaging the active Mederi partner relationship
deals.next_step: Low-touch follow-up only; explore internally whether a modified ClearPay model for recurring patients is worth building.
deals.key_notes: Near-dead — owner rejected ClearPay as poor fit; Mederi relationship limits how hard to push.

deals.name: OS1 Orthopedic & Sports Injury Clinics
deals.champion: Randy Johansen (board member, former AFC CEO)
deals.status: Very early. Randy was introduced to Brantley at the UCA happy hour on Apr 20. OS1 operates under an urgent ortho model, which is ICP-aligned. Only a brief conversation so far — a follow-up meeting was discussed for after UCA. No discovery details yet.
key_risks: Extremely early — no discovery or pain validation done
Randy is retired and casually engaged; unclear if he is an active internal champion
deals.next_step: Brantley to schedule post-UCA discovery call with Randy.
deals.key_notes: Warm intro at UCA; urgent ortho model is ICP-aligned but zero discovery done.

deals.name: Plymouth Bay Ortho
deals.size: 1 standalone ortho urgent care location (June 1 opening)
deals.champion: Christopher LaFave (Executive Director); Jennifer Hazelton (Dir. of Marketing)
deals.status: Internal Solv no-go decision made Apr 22. Prospect showed strong interest through a positive Apr 8 demo with full leadership team and verbal buy-in. However, the opportunity was removed from active pipeline — Solv will not build a custom ECW integration for a single urgent care location. Prospect rejection was not the driver; this was a Solv-side resource and ICP decision.
key_risks: ECW build investment not justified for single-location urgent care
Referral workflow gap would have blocked expansion to other 3 ortho clinics anyway
deals.next_step: No action — revisit only if scope expands to include all 4 locations or ECW build economics change.
deals.key_notes: Internal no-go Apr 22 — ECW build not worth single location; prospect was interested but Solv chose not to pursue.

leadership_table.key_notes: Pricing approved; dual-fee sticker shock on Apr 23 call — finance flexibility decision in 1–2 days.
Free pilot offer sent; Jim needs defensible ROI model before he'll commit to partners.
Won't replace custom scheduler; acquisition-only model needs internal Solv go/no-go.
Paused — Assort contract blocking movement; Jon plans to return when clear, no timeline.
Near-dead — owner rejected ClearPay; Mederi relationship limits outreach pressure.
Warm intro at UCA only; urgent ortho ICP fit but zero discovery done.
Internal no-go Apr 22 — ECW build not justified for single location; prospect was warm.
